sevenius.nilsen: May I ask, things fell apart after your C8 invasion.  What would you have done in my situation? Or, was my c10 stone bad or the c6 group?

ExNihilo: I would say the problem stems from F6, actually. I'm not too familiar with it, but it's typically a move you play when you don't have the room for a proper extension on the left side and want to keep your stones light.
ExNihilo: D6 or D7 instead is normal, and is solid enough to prevent a white invasion after you take your extension (at C10 if you play D6, for instance))

ExNihilo: As for what to do against white C8, in order to remain consistent with the light stone at F8, perhaps just ignore ? The AI suggests a counter-attack at L4, which does look like the important direction.

sevenius.nilsen: I see. Thank you. I played d6 because I was thinking you'd pincer me with c8 or c9 which would make me run, giving you c13 for example and a group to attack later. Perhaps I could have pincered back with c12 for example. I get confused with such joseki, feeling D6 being too thick and slow
ExNihilo: Well, white is free to pincer if you play D6, but you can lean on the corner at E3 and get a good position towards the lower side, so there is no risk that you're forced to run
ExNihilo: You can take a look at this for the continuation : https://ps.waltheri.net/877747
